description: A wide (1200x675) designed 'On this day' anniversary/infographic card commemorating the 12th anniversary of DACA. The left two-thirds is a clean off-white/pale-blue background carrying bold black text: a large serif headline 'On this day,' followed by sans serif body text 'In 2012, President Obama launched DACA (Deferred Action for Childhood Arrivals).' Along the bottom runs a horizontal timeline graphic — a multicolor (yellow-to-green-to-blue gradient) line punctuated by open circular node markers, evoking a chronological milestone. The right third is an angled photographic inset showing President Barack Obama (in a dark suit) standing in the Oval Office beside a young woman in a black blazer and pink top, with another person in the background; the historical photo references the program's launch. The blue-and-white U.S. Department of Homeland Security seal sits in the lower-right corner over the photo. Style: editorial 'this day in history' card mixing flat infographic timeline design with a documentary photo. Intent: celebrate DACA recipients and frame the program positively on its anniversary (Biden-era DHS messaging).
notable_text: On this day, In 2012, President Obama launched DACA (Deferred Action for Childhood Arrivals).
